texto: To love is a natural instinct. To be loved is “something”. To be loved like crazy, like their life depends on you is a once-in-a-lifetime feeling. How many of us can keep their right hand on their heart and say that they have actually experienced something like that? Not many, I guess. Because you know what, once-in-a-lifetime moments, well, come once in a lifetime. You either have to extremely, enormously and tremendously lucky or have to manage to fascinate a poet or a painter or someone really very naïve or mentally unsound.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Daya Kudari: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. O trecho “To love is a natural instinct. To be loved is “something”. To be loved like crazy, like their lif…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que amor, love, luck pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de termino e luck.

frase_original: To love is a natural instinct. To be loved is “something”. To be loved like crazy, like their life depends on you is a once-in-a-lifetime feeling. How many of us can keep their right hand on their heart and say that they have actually experienced something like that? Not many, I guess. Because you know what, once-in-a-lifetime moments, well, come once in a lifetime. You either have to extremely, enormously and tremendously lucky or have to manage to fascinate a poet or a painter or someone really very naïve or mentally unsound.
